Key findings:
1. **Project Name**: Pense - Voice Agent Simulation and Evaluation Platform
2. **Tech Stack**: Next.js 16.1.1, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, NextAuth.js (beta), Recharts
3. **Architecture**: App Router with client components, REST API backend
4. **Authentication**: Google OAuth via NextAuth.js

**Main Sections/Features**:
- **Agents**: CRUD operations for voice agents with configurable STT/TTS/LLM providers
- **Tools**: Function calling tools that agents can use
- **Unit Tests**: STT and TTS evaluation (comparing providers)
- **End-to-End Tests**: 
  - Personas (simulated user characteristics)
  - Scenarios (task definitions)
  - Metrics (evaluation criteria)
  - Simulations (full agent testing with personas + scenarios)

**Component Patterns**:
- AppLayout for consistent sidebar navigation
- Tab-based interfaces for complex pages
- Dialog/Modal patterns for forms
- Sidebar panels for create/edit forms
- Toast notifications for success feedback
- Loading spinners and error states

**API Patterns**:
- REST API at NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL
- Standard CRUD operations: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
- Include `ngrok-skip-browser-warning: true` header
- JSON request/response bodies

**Styling Patterns**:
- CSS variables for theming (light/dark mode)
- Tailwind utility classes
- Custom scrollbar styling
- Consistent spacing and sizing

Let me now write the comprehensive documentation.</details></think>

```diff
@@ -3,3 +3,337 @@
  alwaysApply: true
  ---
+ 
+ # Pense Frontend
+ 
+ **Voice Agent Simulation and Evaluation Platform**
+ 
+ Pense is a comprehensive platform for creating, configuring, testing, and evaluating voice agents. It provides tools for unit testing individual components (STT, TTS) and end-to-end simulation testing with configurable personas and scenarios.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tech Stack
+ 
+ - **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.1 with App Router
+ - **React**: 19.2.3
+ - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 with CSS variables for theming
+ - **Authentication**: NextAuth.js v5 (beta) with Google OAuth
+ - **Charts**: Recharts 3.6.0
+ - **TypeScript**: 5.x
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Project Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ src/
+ ├── app/                    # Next.js App Router pages
+ │   ├── agents/            # Agent management (list + [uuid] detail)
+ │   ├── tools/             # Tools management
+ │   ├── stt/               # Speech-to-Text evaluation
+ │   ├── tts/               # Text-to-Speech evaluation
+ │   ├── tests/             # Tests page
+ │   ├── personas/          # User persona definitions
+ │   ├── scenarios/         # Test scenario definitions
+ │   ├── metrics/           # Evaluation metrics
+ │   ├── simulations/       # End-to-end simulation testing
+ │   ├── login/             # Authentication page
+ │   └── api/auth/          # NextAuth.js route handlers
+ ├── components/
+ │   ├── agent-tabs/        # Agent detail tab components
+ │   ├── evaluations/       # Evaluation UI components
+ │   ├── charts/            # Recharts visualization components
+ │   ├── providers/         # React context providers
+ │   └── simulation-tabs/   # Simulation detail components
+ ├── constants/             # Static configuration data
+ └── auth.ts               # NextAuth.js configuration
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Architecture Patterns
+ 
+ ### Page Structure
+ 
+ All pages follow a consistent structure:
+ - Use &#96;"use client"&#96; directive (client components)
+ - Wrap content in &#96;AppLayout&#96; for sidebar navigation
+ - Manage &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; state locally
+ - Use &#96;useRouter&#96; for navigation
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ export default function ExamplePage() {
+   const router = useRouter();
+   const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);
+ 
+   return (
+     &lt;AppLayout
+       activeItem="page-name"
+       onItemChange={(itemId) =&gt; router.push(&#96;/${itemId}&#96;)}
+       sidebarOpen={sidebarOpen}
+       onSidebarToggle={() =&gt; setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)}
+     &gt;
+       {/* Page content */}
+     &lt;/AppLayout&gt;
+   );
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Component Patterns
+ 
+ 1. **Tab Navigation**: Used in agent detail and simulation detail pages
+ 2. **Sidebar Panels**: Slide-in panels for create/edit forms (40% width, min 500px)
+ 3. **Modal Dialogs**: Centered overlays for confirmations and simple forms
+ 4. **Delete Confirmation**: Reusable &#96;DeleteConfirmationDialog&#96; component
+ 5. **Toast Notifications**: Bottom-right success toasts with auto-dismiss
+ 
+ ### Data Fetching Pattern
+ 
+ - Fetch data in &#96;useEffect&#96; with loading/error states
+ - Backend URL from &#96;process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;
+ - Always include headers:
+   &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+   headers: {
+     accept: "application/json",
+     "Content-Type": "application/json",  // for POST/PUT
+     "ngrok-skip-browser-warning": "true",
+   }
+   &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ - Handle loading spinners and error states consistently
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Styling Guidelines
+ 
+ ### CSS Variables (defined in globals.css)
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;css
+ :root {
+   --background: #ffffff;
+   --foreground: #1a1a1a;
+   --muted: #f5f5f5;
+   --muted-foreground: #737373;
+   --border: #e5e5e5;
+   --accent: #f5f5f5;
+   --accent-foreground: #171717;
+   --sidebar-width: 260px;
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Dark mode is automatically applied via &#96;prefers-color-scheme: dark&#96;.
+ 
+ ### Component Sizing
+ 
+ - **Standard heights**: &#96;h-8&#96; (32px), &#96;h-9&#96; (36px), &#96;h-10&#96; (40px), &#96;h-11&#96; (44px)
+ - **Text sizes**: &#96;text-[12px]&#96;, &#96;text-[13px]&#96;, &#96;text-[14px]&#96;, &#96;text-[15px]&#96;, &#96;text-base&#96;, &#96;text-lg&#96;, &#96;text-2xl&#96;
+ - **Border radius**: &#96;rounded-md&#96; (buttons/inputs), &#96;rounded-xl&#96; (cards/panels)
+ - **Spacing**: Use Tailwind spacing (e.g., &#96;gap-2&#96;, &#96;gap-3&#96;, &#96;gap-4&#96;, &#96;p-4&#96;, &#96;p-6&#96;, &#96;p-8&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Button Styles
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ // Primary button
+ className="h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base font-medium bg-foreground text-background hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity cursor-pointer"
+ 
+ // Secondary button
+ className="h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base font-medium border border-border bg-background hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors cursor-pointer"
+ 
+ // Danger/Delete button
+ className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-red-500 hover:bg-red-500/10"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Input Styles
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ className="w-full h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base border border-border bg-background text-foreground placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent focus:border-transparent"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Domain Concepts
+ 
+ ### Agents
+ 
+ Voice agents configured with:
+ - **System Prompt**: Defines agent persona and behavior
+ - **STT Provider**: Speech-to-text service (google, openai, deepgram, etc.)
+ - **TTS Provider**: Text-to-speech service (google, openai, cartesia, etc.)
+ - **LLM Model**: Language model (organized by provider: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, etc.)
+ - **Tools**: Function calling tools the agent can use
+ - **Data Extraction Fields**: Fields to extract from conversations
+ - **Settings**: Agent speaks first, end conversation tool enabled
+ 
+ ### Tools
+ 
+ Custom function calling tools with:
+ - **Name**: Function name for LLM
+ - **Description**: When/how to use the tool
+ - **Parameters**: JSON Schema formatted parameters (supports nested objects, arrays)
+ 
+ ### Personas
+ 
+ Simulated user characteristics:
+ - **Label**: Persona name
+ - **Characteristics**: Detailed description of WHO they are and HOW they behave
+ - **Gender**: male | female
+ - **Language**: english | hindi | kannada
+ - **Interruption Sensitivity**: none | low | medium | high
+ 
+ ### Scenarios
+ 
+ Test scenarios defining WHAT the persona should do:
+ - **Label**: Scenario name
+ - **Description**: Task or conversation goal
+ 
+ ### Simulations
+ 
+ End-to-end tests combining:
+ - Agent configuration
+ - Selected personas
+ - Selected scenarios
+ - Evaluation metrics
+ - Run types: chat | audio | voice
+ 
+ ### Evaluations
+ 
+ Unit test evaluations for:
+ - **STT**: Upload audio + transcription, compare providers
+ - **TTS**: Upload text, compare provider outputs
+ - Metrics: WER, String Similarity, LLM Judge Score, TTFB, Processing Time
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## API Endpoints
+ 
+ All endpoints are relative to &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;:
+ 
+ | Resource | Endpoints |
+ |----------|-----------|
+ | Agents | &#96;GET/POST /agents&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /agents/{uuid}&#96;, &#96;POST /agents/{uuid}/duplicate&#96; |
+ | Agent Tools | &#96;GET /agent-tools/agent/{uuid}/tools&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tools&#96; |
+ | Tools | &#96;GET/POST /tools&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tools/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Personas | &#96;GET/POST /personas&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /personas/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Scenarios | &#96;GET/POST /scenarios&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /scenarios/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Simulations | &#96;GET/POST /simulations&#96;, &#96;GET/DELETE /simulations/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Simulation Runs | &#96;GET /simulations/run/{runId}&#96;, &#96;POST /simulations/{uuid}/run&#96; |
+ | Tests | &#96;GET/POST /tests&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tests/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Agent Tests | &#96;GET /agent-tests/agent/{uuid}/tests&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tests&#96; |
+ | STT Evaluation | &#96;POST /stt/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /stt/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
+ | TTS Evaluation | &#96;POST /tts/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /tts/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
+ | Presigned URLs | &#96;POST /presigned-url&#96; |
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Common Patterns
+ 
+ ### Loading State
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ {isLoading ? (
+   &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-center gap-3 py-8"&gt;
+     &lt;svg className="w-5 h-5 animate-spin" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24"&gt;
+       &lt;circle className="opacity-25" cx="12" cy="12" r="10" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="4" /&gt;
+       &lt;path className="opacity-75" fill="currentColor" d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z" /&gt;
+     &lt;/svg&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+ ) : /* content */}
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Empty State
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl p-12 flex flex-col items-center justify-center bg-muted/20"&gt;
+   &lt;div className="w-14 h-14 rounded-xl bg-muted flex items-center justify-center mb-4"&gt;
+     {/* Icon */}
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+   &lt;h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-foreground mb-1"&gt;No items found&lt;/h3&gt;
+   &lt;p className="text-base text-muted-foreground mb-4"&gt;Description&lt;/p&gt;
+   &lt;button&gt;Add item&lt;/button&gt;
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Table Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl overflow-hidden"&gt;
+   {/* Header */}
+   &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border bg-muted/30"&gt;
+     &lt;div className="text-sm font-medium text-muted-foreground"&gt;Column&lt;/div&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+   {/* Rows */}
+   {items.map((item) =&gt; (
+     &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border last:border-b-0 hover:bg-muted/20 transition-colors cursor-pointer"&gt;
+       {/* Content */}
+     &lt;/div&gt;
+   ))}
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Polling for Async Tasks
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ useEffect(() =&gt; {
+   let pollInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
+   
+   const fetchData = async (isInitial = false) =&gt; {
+     // ... fetch
+     if (data.status === "done" &amp;&amp; pollInterval) {
+       clearInterval(pollInterval);
+     }
+   };
+   
+   fetchData(true);
+   pollInterval = setInterval(() =&gt; fetchData(false), 3000);
+   
+   return () =&gt; {
+     if (pollInterval) clearInterval(pollInterval);
+   };
+ }, [dependency]);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Navigation Structure
+ 
+ ### Sidebar Sections
+ 
+ 1. **Main**
+    - Agents
+    - Tools
+ 
+ 2. **Unit Tests**
+    - Speech-to-Text (STT)
+    - Text-to-Speech (TTS)
+    - Tests
+ 
+ 3. **End-to-End Tests**
+    - Personas
+    - Scenarios
+    - Metrics
+    - Simulations
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Environment Variables
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000  # Backend API URL
+ AUTH_SECRET=                                    # NextAuth secret
+ GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=                              # Google OAuth client ID
+ GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=                          # Google OAuth client secret
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Coding Standards
+ 
+ 1. **Components**: Use functional components with hooks
+ 2. **State**: Use &#96;useState&#96; for local state, avoid global state management
+ 3. **Types**: Define TypeScript types at component top
+ 4. **Naming**: 
+    - Components: PascalCase
+    - Functions: camelCase
+    - Types: PascalCase with descriptive suffix (e.g., &#96;AgentData&#96;, &#96;ToolData&#96;)
+ 5. **Files**: One main component per file, helper components at bottom
+ 6. **Icons**: Use inline SVG from Heroicons (outline style, 24x24 viewBox)
+ 7. **Error Handling**: Try-catch with console.error and user-facing error states
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

I've thoroughly analyzed the project and documented everything in `.cursor/rules/app-details.md`. Here's a summary of what the documentation covers:

## Project Overview
**Pense** is a Voice Agent Simulation and Evaluation Platform built with:
- Next.js 16.1.1 (App Router)
- React 19
- Tailwind CSS 4
- NextAuth.js v5 with Google OAuth
- Recharts for visualizations

## Key Documentation Sections

### Architecture Patterns
- Standard page structure with `AppLayout` wrapper
- Tab-based navigation for complex pages
- Slide-in sidebar panels for forms
- Modal dialogs for confirmations
- Consistent data fetching with loading/error states

### Domain Concepts
- **Agents**: Voice agents with STT/TTS/LLM configuration
- **Tools**: Function calling tools with JSON Schema parameters  
- **Personas**: Simulated user characteristics (gender, language, interruption sensitivity)
- **Scenarios**: Test scenarios defining conversation goals
- **Simulations**: End-to-end tests combining agents, personas, and scenarios
- **Evaluations**: Unit tests for STT/TTS with metrics (WER, TTFB, etc.)

### Styling Guidelines
- CSS variables for light/dark theming
- Consistent component sizing and spacing
- Standardized button and input styles
- Tailwind utility class patterns

### Coding Standards
- Functional components with hooks
- TypeScript types at component top
- PascalCase for components/types, camelCase for functions
- Inline SVG icons from Heroicons
- Error handling with try-catch and user-facing states

### API Reference
Complete endpoint documentation for all backend resources (agents, tools, personas, scenarios, simulations, tests, evaluations).

```diff
+ ## What is Pense?
...
- ## Tech Stack
+ Pense is a comprehensive platform for building, configuring, testing, and evaluating **voice-based AI agents**. It enables teams to create conversational agents that can handle voice interactions (phone calls, voice assistants) and rigorously test them before deployment.
...
- - **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.1 with App Router
- - **React**: 19.2.3
- - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 with CSS variables for theming
- - **Authentication**: NextAuth.js v5 (beta) with Google OAuth
- - **Charts**: Recharts 3.6.0
- - **TypeScript**: 5.x
-

- 
- ## Project Structure
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- src/
- ├── app/                    # Next.js App Router pages
- │   ├── agents/            # Agent management (list + [uuid] detail)
- │   ├── tools/             # Tools management
- │   ├── stt/               # Speech-to-Text evaluation
- │   ├── tts/               # Text-to-Speech evaluation

- │   ├── personas/          # User persona definitions
- │   ├── scenarios/         # Test scenario definitions
- │   ├── metrics/           # Evaluation metrics
- │   ├── simulations/       # End-to-end simulation testing

- ├── components/
- │   ├── agent-tabs/        # Agent detail tab components
- │   ├── evaluations/       # Evaluation UI components
- │   ├── charts/            # Recharts visualization components
- │   ├── providers/         # React context providers
- │   └── simulation-tabs/   # Simulation detail components
- ├── constants/             # Static configuration data
- └── auth.ts               # NextAuth.js configuration

- ## Architecture Patterns
- 
- ### Page Structure
- 
- All pages follow a consistent structure:
- - Use &#96;"use client"&#96; directive (client components)
- - Wrap content in &#96;AppLayout&#96; for sidebar navigation
- - Manage &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; state locally
- - Use &#96;useRouter&#96; for navigation
+ The platform addresses the challenge of quality assurance for voice agents by providing:
+ - **Component-level testing** (unit tests for STT/TTS providers)
+ - **End-to-end simulation testing** (full conversations with simulated users)
+ - **Benchmarking** across different AI providers to find the best configuration
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Overall Context &amp; Use Cases
+ 
+ ### Primary Use Case
+ Organizations building voice agents (customer support bots, IVR systems, voice assistants) use Pense to:
+ 1. Configure their agent's voice pipeline (which STT, TTS, and LLM to use)
+ 2. Test individual components to select the best providers
+ 3. Run simulated conversations to validate agent behavior
+ 4. Evaluate agent performance with metrics before going live
+ 
+ ### Workflow Overview
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+ │                           PENSE WORKFLOW                                │
+ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+ │                                                                         │
+ │   1. CREATE AGENT                                                       │
+ │      └── Configure: System Prompt, STT, TTS, LLM, Tools                 │
+ │                                                                         │
+ │   2. UNIT TEST (Optional)                                               │
+ │      ├── STT Evaluation: Compare speech-to-text providers               │
+ │      └── TTS Evaluation: Compare text-to-speech providers               │
+ │                                                                         │
+ │   3. SETUP END-TO-END TESTS                                             │
+ │      ├── Create Personas: Define simulated user characteristics         │
+ │      ├── Create Scenarios: Define conversation goals/tasks              │
+ │      └── Define Metrics: Set evaluation criteria                        │
+ │                                                                         │
+ │   4. RUN SIMULATIONS                                                    │
+ │      ├── Create Simulation: Select agent + personas + scenarios         │
+ │      ├── Execute Run: System simulates conversations                    │
+ │      └── Review Results: Transcripts, metrics, pass/fail status         │
+ │                                                                         │
+ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Complete Feature List

+ ### 1. Agent Management (&#96;/agents&#96;)

+ **What you can do:**

+ - **Edit agent configuration** including:

+ ### 2. Tools Management (&#96;/tools&#96;)
...
- 1. **Tab Navigation**: Used in agent detail and simulation detail pages
- 2. **Sidebar Panels**: Slide-in panels for create/edit forms (40% width, min 500px)
- 3. **Modal Dialogs**: Centered overlays for confirmations and simple forms
- 4. **Delete Confirmation**: Reusable &#96;DeleteConfirmationDialog&#96; component
- 5. **Toast Notifications**: Bottom-right success toasts with auto-dismiss
- 
- ### Data Fetching Pattern
- 
- - Fetch data in &#96;useEffect&#96; with loading/error states
- - Backend URL from &#96;process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;
- - Always include headers:
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
-   headers: {
-     accept: "application/json",
-     "Content-Type": "application/json",  // for POST/PUT
-     "ngrok-skip-browser-warning": "true",
-   }
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
- - Handle loading spinners and error states consistently
-

- ## Styling Guidelines
- 
- ### CSS Variables (defined in globals.css)
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;css
- :root {
-   --background: #ffffff;

-   --border: #e5e5e5;
-   --accent: #f5f5f5;

- &#96;&#96;&#96;

- 
- - **Standard heights**: &#96;h-8&#96; (32px), &#96;h-9&#96; (36px), &#96;h-10&#96; (40px), &#96;h-11&#96; (44px)
- - **Text sizes**: &#96;text-[12px]&#96;, &#96;text-[13px]&#96;, &#96;text-[14px]&#96;, &#96;text-[15px]&#96;, &#96;text-base&#96;, &#96;text-lg&#96;, &#96;text-2xl&#96;
- - **Border radius**: &#96;rounded-md&#96; (buttons/inputs), &#96;rounded-xl&#96; (cards/panels)

- &#96;&#96;&#96;

- ---
- 
- ## Domain Concepts
- 
- ### Agents
- 
- Voice agents configured with:
- - **System Prompt**: Defines agent persona and behavior
- - **STT Provider**: Speech-to-text service (google, openai, deepgram, etc.)
- - **TTS Provider**: Text-to-speech service (google, openai, cartesia, etc.)
- - **LLM Model**: Language model (organized by provider: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, etc.)
- - **Tools**: Function calling tools the agent can use
- - **Data Extraction Fields**: Fields to extract from conversations
- - **Settings**: Agent speaks first, end conversation tool enabled
- 
- ### Tools
- 
- Custom function calling tools with:
- - **Name**: Function name for LLM
- - **Description**: When/how to use the tool
- - **Parameters**: JSON Schema formatted parameters (supports nested objects, arrays)
- 
- ### Personas
- 
- Simulated user characteristics:
- - **Label**: Persona name
- - **Characteristics**: Detailed description of WHO they are and HOW they behave
- - **Gender**: male | female
- - **Language**: english | hindi | kannada
- - **Interruption Sensitivity**: none | low | medium | high
- 
- ### Scenarios
- 
- Test scenarios defining WHAT the persona should do:
- - **Label**: Scenario name
- - **Description**: Task or conversation goal
- 
- ### Simulations
- 
- End-to-end tests combining:
- - Agent configuration
- - Selected personas
- - Selected scenarios
- - Evaluation metrics
- - Run types: chat | audio | voice
- 
- ### Evaluations
- 
- Unit test evaluations for:
- - **STT**: Upload audio + transcription, compare providers
- - **TTS**: Upload text, compare provider outputs
- - Metrics: WER, String Similarity, LLM Judge Score, TTFB, Processing Time
- 
- ---
- 
- ## API Endpoints
- 
- All endpoints are relative to &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;:
- 
- | Resource | Endpoints |
- |----------|-----------|
- | Agents | &#96;GET/POST /agents&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /agents/{uuid}&#96;, &#96;POST /agents/{uuid}/duplicate&#96; |
- | Agent Tools | &#96;GET /agent-tools/agent/{uuid}/tools&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tools&#96; |
- | Tools | &#96;GET/POST /tools&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tools/{uuid}&#96; |
- | Personas | &#96;GET/POST /personas&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /personas/{uuid}&#96; |
- | Scenarios | &#96;GET/POST /scenarios&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /scenarios/{uuid}&#96; |
- | Simulations | &#96;GET/POST /simulations&#96;, &#96;GET/DELETE /simulations/{uuid}&#96; |
- | Simulation Runs | &#96;GET /simulations/run/{runId}&#96;, &#96;POST /simulations/{uuid}/run&#96; |
- | Tests | &#96;GET/POST /tests&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tests/{uuid}&#96; |
- | Agent Tests | &#96;GET /agent-tests/agent/{uuid}/tests&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tests&#96; |
- | STT Evaluation | &#96;POST /stt/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /stt/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
- | TTS Evaluation | &#96;POST /tts/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /tts/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
- | Presigned URLs | &#96;POST /presigned-url&#96; |
- 
- ---
- 
- ## Common Patterns
- 
- ### Loading State
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- {isLoading ? (
-   &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-center gap-3 py-8"&gt;
-     &lt;svg className="w-5 h-5 animate-spin" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24"&gt;
-       &lt;circle className="opacity-25" cx="12" cy="12" r="10" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="4" /&gt;
-       &lt;path className="opacity-75" fill="currentColor" d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z" /&gt;
-     &lt;/svg&gt;
-   &lt;/div&gt;
- ) : /* content */}
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- 
- ### Empty State
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl p-12 flex flex-col items-center justify-center bg-muted/20"&gt;
-   &lt;div className="w-14 h-14 rounded-xl bg-muted flex items-center justify-center mb-4"&gt;
-     {/* Icon */}
-   &lt;/div&gt;
-   &lt;h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-foreground mb-1"&gt;No items found&lt;/h3&gt;
-   &lt;p className="text-base text-muted-foreground mb-4"&gt;Description&lt;/p&gt;
-   &lt;button&gt;Add item&lt;/button&gt;
- &lt;/div&gt;
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- 
- ### Table Structure
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl overflow-hidden"&gt;
-   {/* Header */}
-   &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border bg-muted/30"&gt;
-     &lt;div className="text-sm font-medium text-muted-foreground"&gt;Column&lt;/div&gt;
-   &lt;/div&gt;
-   {/* Rows */}
-   {items.map((item) =&gt; (
-     &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border last:border-b-0 hover:bg-muted/20 transition-colors cursor-pointer"&gt;
-       {/* Content */}
-     &lt;/div&gt;
-   ))}
- &lt;/div&gt;
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- 
- ### Polling for Async Tasks
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- useEffect(() =&gt; {
-   let pollInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
-   
-   const fetchData = async (isInitial = false) =&gt; {
-     // ... fetch
-     if (data.status === "done" &amp;&amp; pollInterval) {
-       clearInterval(pollInterval);
-     }
-   };
-   
-   fetchData(true);
-   pollInterval = setInterval(() =&gt; fetchData(false), 3000);
-   
-   return () =&gt; {
-     if (pollInterval) clearInterval(pollInterval);
-   };
- }, [dependency]);
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- 
- ---
- 
- ## Navigation Structure
- 
- ### Sidebar Sections
- 
- 1. **Main**
-    - Agents
-    - Tools
- 
- 2. **Unit Tests**
-    - Speech-to-Text (STT)
-    - Text-to-Speech (TTS)
-    - Tests

- 3. **End-to-End Tests**
-    - Personas
-    - Scenarios

- ## Environment Variables
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
- NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000  # Backend API URL
- AUTH_SECRET=                                    # NextAuth secret
- GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=                              # Google OAuth client ID
- GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=                          # Google OAuth client secret
- &#96;&#96;&#96;
- 
- ---
- 
- ## Coding Standards
- 
- 1. **Components**: Use functional components with hooks
- 2. **State**: Use &#96;useState&#96; for local state, avoid global state management
- 3. **Types**: Define TypeScript types at component top
- 4. **Naming**: 
-    - Components: PascalCase
-    - Functions: camelCase
-    - Types: PascalCase with descriptive suffix (e.g., &#96;AgentData&#96;, &#96;ToolData&#96;)
- 5. **Files**: One main component per file, helper components at bottom
- 6. **Icons**: Use inline SVG from Heroicons (outline style, 24x24 viewBox)
- 7. **Error Handling**: Try-catch with console.error and user-facing error states
- 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Create custom tools** for LLM function calling
+ - **Define tool parameters** with full JSON Schema support:
+   - Primitive types: string, number, boolean, integer
+   - Complex types: object (with nested properties), array (with item types)
+   - Required/optional flags
+   - Descriptions for each parameter
+ - **Edit existing tools**
+ - **Delete tools**
+ - **Search tools** by name or description
+ 
+ ### 3. Speech-to-Text Evaluation (&#96;/stt&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Upload audio files** (.wav format) with reference transcriptions
+ - **Add multiple test samples** for batch evaluation
+ - **Select providers to evaluate** (compare multiple simultaneously)
+ - **Choose language** (English or Hindi)
+ - **Run evaluation** and get results:
+   - **Leaderboard view** with comparative charts
+   - **Per-provider metrics**: WER, String Similarity, LLM Judge Score, TTFB, Processing Time
+   - **Detailed outputs** showing ground truth vs predictions
+   - **LLM Judge reasoning** explaining semantic accuracy
+ 
+ ### 4. Text-to-Speech Evaluation (&#96;/tts&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Upload text samples** to convert to speech
+ - **Select providers to compare**
+ - **Run evaluation** and compare:
+   - Audio quality
+   - Latency metrics
+   - Provider performance
+ 
+ ### 5. Tests Management (&#96;/tests&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Create test cases** with:
+   - Name and description
+   - Test type: "response" (check agent response) or "tool_call" (check tool invocation)
+   - Test configuration
+ - **View all tests**
+ - **Edit/delete tests**
+ - **Link tests to agents** for benchmarking
+ 
+ ### 6. Personas Management (&#96;/personas&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Create user personas** that define WHO the simulated user is:
+   - **Label**: Persona identifier
+   - **Characteristics**: Detailed personality description (age, background, speaking style, temperament)
+   - **Gender**: Male or Female (affects voice synthesis)
+   - **Language**: English, Hindi, or Kannada
+   - **Interruption Sensitivity**: None, Low, Medium, or High (how likely to interrupt the agent)
+ - **Edit existing personas**
+ - **Delete personas**
+ - **Search personas**
+ 
+ ### 7. Scenarios Management (&#96;/scenarios&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Create test scenarios** that define WHAT the simulated user does:
+   - **Label**: Scenario identifier
+   - **Description**: Task or conversation goal (e.g., "Call to inquire about crop insurance")
+ - **Edit existing scenarios**
+ - **Delete scenarios**
+ - **Search scenarios**
+ 
+ ### 8. Metrics Management (&#96;/metrics&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Define evaluation criteria** for simulations
+ - **Configure pass/fail thresholds**
+ - **Set up custom metrics**
+ 
+ ### 9. Simulations (&#96;/simulations&#96;)
+ 
+ **What you can do:**
+ - **Create simulations** by selecting:
+   - Target agent
+   - Personas to use
+   - Scenarios to test
+   - Evaluation metrics
+ - **Run simulations** in different modes:
+   - **Chat**: Text-based conversation simulation
+   - **Audio**: Audio-based simulation
+   - **Voice**: Full voice pipeline simulation
+ - **View simulation runs** with:
+   - Status tracking (pending, running, done, failed)
+   - Progress indicators
+   - Total simulation count
+ - **Analyze results**:
+   - **Performance metrics**: Tool calls accuracy, Answer completeness, Assistant behavior, Question completeness
+   - **Per-simulation breakdown**: View each persona+scenario combination
+   - **Transcripts**: Full conversation history
+   - **Evaluation scores**: Pass/fail for each metric with reasoning
+ - **Download results** for further analysis
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Key Concepts Explained
+ 
+ ### Voice Agent Pipeline
+ 
+ A voice agent processes conversations through this pipeline:
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ User Speech → [STT] → Text → [LLM] → Response Text → [TTS] → Agent Speech
+                               ↓
+                         [Tool Calls]
+                               ↓
+                        External APIs
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Pense allows testing and benchmarking each component:
+ - **STT (Speech-to-Text)**: Converts user's voice to text
+ - **LLM (Large Language Model)**: Generates intelligent responses
+ - **TTS (Text-to-Speech)**: Converts agent's response to voice
+ - **Tools**: External functions the LLM can call
+ 
+ ### Simulation Testing Approach
+ 
+ Instead of manual testing, Pense uses AI-powered simulation:
+ 1. **Personas** act as synthetic users with defined characteristics
+ 2. **Scenarios** give these personas specific tasks to accomplish
+ 3. The system runs automated conversations between the agent and personas
+ 4. **Metrics** evaluate if the agent handled the conversation correctly
+ 
+ This enables:
+ - Testing edge cases (angry customers, language barriers)
+ - Regression testing after changes
+ - Scale testing (run hundreds of simulations)
+ - Objective evaluation with consistent criteria
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Tech Stack
+ 
+ - **Framework**: Next.js 16.1.1 with App Router
+ - **React**: 19.2.3
+ - **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4 with CSS variables for theming
+ - **Authentication**: NextAuth.js v5 (beta) with Google OAuth
+ - **Charts**: Recharts 3.6.0
+ - **TypeScript**: 5.x
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Project Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ src/
+ ├── app/                    # Next.js App Router pages
+ │   ├── agents/            # Agent management (list + [uuid] detail)
+ │   ├── tools/             # Tools management
+ │   ├── stt/               # Speech-to-Text evaluation
+ │   ├── tts/               # Text-to-Speech evaluation
+ │   ├── tests/             # Tests page
+ │   ├── personas/          # User persona definitions
+ │   ├── scenarios/         # Test scenario definitions
+ │   ├── metrics/           # Evaluation metrics
+ │   ├── simulations/       # End-to-end simulation testing
+ │   ├── login/             # Authentication page
+ │   └── api/auth/          # NextAuth.js route handlers
+ ├── components/
+ │   ├── agent-tabs/        # Agent detail tab components
+ │   ├── evaluations/       # Evaluation UI components
+ │   ├── charts/            # Recharts visualization components
+ │   ├── providers/         # React context providers
+ │   └── simulation-tabs/   # Simulation detail components
+ ├── constants/             # Static configuration data
+ └── auth.ts               # NextAuth.js configuration
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Architecture Patterns
+ 
+ ### Page Structure
+ 
+ All pages follow a consistent structure:
+ - Use &#96;"use client"&#96; directive (client components)
+ - Wrap content in &#96;AppLayout&#96; for sidebar navigation
+ - Manage &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; state locally
+ - Use &#96;useRouter&#96; for navigation
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ export default function ExamplePage() {
+   const router = useRouter();
+   const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);
+ 
+   return (
+     &lt;AppLayout
+       activeItem="page-name"
+       onItemChange={(itemId) =&gt; router.push(&#96;/${itemId}&#96;)}
+       sidebarOpen={sidebarOpen}
+       onSidebarToggle={() =&gt; setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)}
+     &gt;
+       {/* Page content */}
+     &lt;/AppLayout&gt;
+   );
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Component Patterns
+ 
+ 1. **Tab Navigation**: Used in agent detail and simulation detail pages
+ 2. **Sidebar Panels**: Slide-in panels for create/edit forms (40% width, min 500px)
+ 3. **Modal Dialogs**: Centered overlays for confirmations and simple forms
+ 4. **Delete Confirmation**: Reusable &#96;DeleteConfirmationDialog&#96; component
+ 5. **Toast Notifications**: Bottom-right success toasts with auto-dismiss
+ 
+ ### Data Fetching Pattern
+ 
+ - Fetch data in &#96;useEffect&#96; with loading/error states
+ - Backend URL from &#96;process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;
+ - Always include headers:
+   &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+   headers: {
+     accept: "application/json",
+     "Content-Type": "application/json",  // for POST/PUT
+     "ngrok-skip-browser-warning": "true",
+   }
+   &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ - Handle loading spinners and error states consistently
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Styling Guidelines
+ 
+ ### CSS Variables (defined in globals.css)
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;css
+ :root {
+   --background: #ffffff;
+   --foreground: #1a1a1a;
+   --muted: #f5f5f5;
+   --muted-foreground: #737373;
+   --border: #e5e5e5;
+   --accent: #f5f5f5;
+   --accent-foreground: #171717;
+   --sidebar-width: 260px;
+ }
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Dark mode is automatically applied via &#96;prefers-color-scheme: dark&#96;.
+ 
+ ### Component Sizing
+ 
+ - **Standard heights**: &#96;h-8&#96; (32px), &#96;h-9&#96; (36px), &#96;h-10&#96; (40px), &#96;h-11&#96; (44px)
+ - **Text sizes**: &#96;text-[12px]&#96;, &#96;text-[13px]&#96;, &#96;text-[14px]&#96;, &#96;text-[15px]&#96;, &#96;text-base&#96;, &#96;text-lg&#96;, &#96;text-2xl&#96;
+ - **Border radius**: &#96;rounded-md&#96; (buttons/inputs), &#96;rounded-xl&#96; (cards/panels)
+ - **Spacing**: Use Tailwind spacing (e.g., &#96;gap-2&#96;, &#96;gap-3&#96;, &#96;gap-4&#96;, &#96;p-4&#96;, &#96;p-6&#96;, &#96;p-8&#96;)
+ 
+ ### Button Styles
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ // Primary button
+ className="h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base font-medium bg-foreground text-background hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity cursor-pointer"
+ 
+ // Secondary button
+ className="h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base font-medium border border-border bg-background hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors cursor-pointer"
+ 
+ // Danger/Delete button
+ className="text-muted-foreground hover:text-red-500 hover:bg-red-500/10"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Input Styles
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ className="w-full h-10 px-4 rounded-md text-base border border-border bg-background text-foreground placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-accent focus:border-transparent"
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Domain Concepts
+ 
+ ### Agents
+ 
+ Voice agents configured with:
+ - **System Prompt**: Defines agent persona and behavior
+ - **STT Provider**: Speech-to-text service (google, openai, deepgram, etc.)
+ - **TTS Provider**: Text-to-speech service (google, openai, cartesia, etc.)
+ - **LLM Model**: Language model (organized by provider: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, etc.)
+ - **Tools**: Function calling tools the agent can use
+ - **Data Extraction Fields**: Fields to extract from conversations
+ - **Settings**: Agent speaks first, end conversation tool enabled
+ 
+ ### Tools
+ 
+ Custom function calling tools with:
+ - **Name**: Function name for LLM
+ - **Description**: When/how to use the tool
+ - **Parameters**: JSON Schema formatted parameters (supports nested objects, arrays)
+ 
+ ### Personas
+ 
+ Simulated user characteristics:
+ - **Label**: Persona name
+ - **Characteristics**: Detailed description of WHO they are and HOW they behave
+ - **Gender**: male | female
+ - **Language**: english | hindi | kannada
+ - **Interruption Sensitivity**: none | low | medium | high
+ 
+ ### Scenarios
+ 
+ Test scenarios defining WHAT the persona should do:
+ - **Label**: Scenario name
+ - **Description**: Task or conversation goal
+ 
+ ### Simulations
+ 
+ End-to-end tests combining:
+ - Agent configuration
+ - Selected personas
+ - Selected scenarios
+ - Evaluation metrics
+ - Run types: chat | audio | voice
+ 
+ ### Evaluations
+ 
+ Unit test evaluations for:
+ - **STT**: Upload audio + transcription, compare providers
+ - **TTS**: Upload text, compare provider outputs
+ - Metrics: WER, String Similarity, LLM Judge Score, TTFB, Processing Time
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## API Endpoints
+ 
+ All endpoints are relative to &#96;NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL&#96;:
+ 
+ | Resource | Endpoints |
+ |----------|-----------|
+ | Agents | &#96;GET/POST /agents&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /agents/{uuid}&#96;, &#96;POST /agents/{uuid}/duplicate&#96; |
+ | Agent Tools | &#96;GET /agent-tools/agent/{uuid}/tools&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tools&#96; |
+ | Tools | &#96;GET/POST /tools&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tools/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Personas | &#96;GET/POST /personas&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /personas/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Scenarios | &#96;GET/POST /scenarios&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /scenarios/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Simulations | &#96;GET/POST /simulations&#96;, &#96;GET/DELETE /simulations/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Simulation Runs | &#96;GET /simulations/run/{runId}&#96;, &#96;POST /simulations/{uuid}/run&#96; |
+ | Tests | &#96;GET/POST /tests&#96;, &#96;GET/PUT/DELETE /tests/{uuid}&#96; |
+ | Agent Tests | &#96;GET /agent-tests/agent/{uuid}/tests&#96;, &#96;POST/DELETE /agent-tests&#96; |
+ | STT Evaluation | &#96;POST /stt/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /stt/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
+ | TTS Evaluation | &#96;POST /tts/evaluate&#96;, &#96;GET /tts/evaluate/{taskId}&#96; |
+ | Presigned URLs | &#96;POST /presigned-url&#96; |
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Common Patterns
+ 
+ ### Loading State
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ {isLoading ? (
+   &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-center gap-3 py-8"&gt;
+     &lt;svg className="w-5 h-5 animate-spin" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24"&gt;
+       &lt;circle className="opacity-25" cx="12" cy="12" r="10" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="4" /&gt;
+       &lt;path className="opacity-75" fill="currentColor" d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4zm2 5.291A7.962 7.962 0 014 12H0c0 3.042 1.135 5.824 3 7.938l3-2.647z" /&gt;
+     &lt;/svg&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+ ) : /* content */}
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Empty State
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl p-12 flex flex-col items-center justify-center bg-muted/20"&gt;
+   &lt;div className="w-14 h-14 rounded-xl bg-muted flex items-center justify-center mb-4"&gt;
+     {/* Icon */}
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+   &lt;h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-foreground mb-1"&gt;No items found&lt;/h3&gt;
+   &lt;p className="text-base text-muted-foreground mb-4"&gt;Description&lt;/p&gt;
+   &lt;button&gt;Add item&lt;/button&gt;
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Table Structure
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ &lt;div className="border border-border rounded-xl overflow-hidden"&gt;
+   {/* Header */}
+   &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border bg-muted/30"&gt;
+     &lt;div className="text-sm font-medium text-muted-foreground"&gt;Column&lt;/div&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+   {/* Rows */}
+   {items.map((item) =&gt; (
+     &lt;div className="grid grid-cols-[...] gap-4 px-4 py-2 border-b border-border last:border-b-0 hover:bg-muted/20 transition-colors cursor-pointer"&gt;
+       {/* Content */}
+     &lt;/div&gt;
+   ))}
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Polling for Async Tasks
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ useEffect(() =&gt; {
+   let pollInterval: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
+   
+   const fetchData = async (isInitial = false) =&gt; {
+     // ... fetch
+     if (data.status === "done" &amp;&amp; pollInterval) {
+       clearInterval(pollInterval);
+     }
+   };
+   
+   fetchData(true);
+   pollInterval = setInterval(() =&gt; fetchData(false), 3000);
+   
+   return () =&gt; {
+     if (pollInterval) clearInterval(pollInterval);
+   };
+ }, [dependency]);
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Navigation Structure
+ 
+ ### Sidebar Sections
+ 
+ 1. **Main**
+    - Agents
+    - Tools
+ 
+ 2. **Unit Tests**
+    - Speech-to-Text (STT)
+    - Text-to-Speech (TTS)
+    - Tests
+ 
+ 3. **End-to-End Tests**
+    - Personas
+    - Scenarios
+    - Metrics
+    - Simulations
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Environment Variables
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
+ NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8000  # Backend API URL
+ AUTH_SECRET=                                    # NextAuth secret
+ GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=                              # Google OAuth client ID
+ GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=                          # Google OAuth client secret
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ ## Coding Standards
+ 
+ 1. **Components**: Use functional components with hooks
+ 2. **State**: Use &#96;useState&#96; for local state, avoid global state management
+ 3. **Types**: Define TypeScript types at component top
+ 4. **Naming**: 
+    - Components: PascalCase
+    - Functions: camelCase
+    - Types: PascalCase with descriptive suffix (e.g., &#96;AgentData&#96;, &#96;ToolData&#96;)
+ 5. **Files**: One main component per file, helper components at bottom
+ 6. **Icons**: Use inline SVG from Heroicons (outline style, 24x24 viewBox)
+ 7. **Error Handling**: Try-catch with console.error and user-facing error states
+ 
```

</details>

</tool-use>

I've expanded the beginning of the documentation with comprehensive details. Here's what I added:

## New Sections Added

### 1. **What is Pense?**
Clear explanation of the platform's purpose - building, configuring, testing, and evaluating voice-based AI agents with component-level and end-to-end testing capabilities.

### 2. **Overall Context & Use Cases**
- Primary use case for organizations building voice agents
- Visual ASCII workflow diagram showing the 4-step process:
  1. Create Agent → 2. Unit Test → 3. Setup E2E Tests → 4. Run Simulations

### 3. **Complete Feature List**
Detailed breakdown of all 9 main features with specific capabilities:

| Section | Key Capabilities |
|---------|-----------------|
| **Agent Management** | Create, configure STT/TTS/LLM, manage tools, data extraction, tests, benchmarks |
| **Tools Management** | Create function calling tools with full JSON Schema parameter support |
| **STT Evaluation** | Upload audio, compare providers, leaderboard with WER/TTFB/LLM Judge metrics |
| **TTS Evaluation** | Compare text-to-speech providers |
| **Tests Management** | Create response and tool_call test cases |
| **Personas** | Define simulated users with characteristics, gender, language, interruption sensitivity |
| **Scenarios** | Define conversation goals and tasks |
| **Metrics** | Configure evaluation criteria |
| **Simulations** | Run chat/audio/voice simulations, view transcripts, analyze results |

### 4. **Key Concepts Explained**
- **Voice Agent Pipeline**: Visual diagram showing User Speech → STT → LLM → TTS → Agent Speech
- **Simulation Testing Approach**: Explanation of how personas + scenarios + metrics enable automated testing at scale

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,78 @@
- 
+ # Terms of Service
+ 
+ **Last Updated:** January 2025
+ 
+ ## 1. Acceptance of Terms
+ 
+ By accessing or using Pense ("the Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, do not use the Platform.
+ 
+ ## 2. Description of Service
+ 
+ Pense is a voice agent simulation and evaluation platform that allows users to:
+ 
+ - Create and configure voice-based AI agents
+ - Test speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) providers
+ - Run simulated conversations for agent evaluation
+ - Analyze agent performance metrics
+ 
+ ## 3. User Accounts
+ 
+ - You must authenticate using Google OAuth to access the Platform
+ - You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account
+ - You must provide accurate information during registration
+ - You may not share your account credentials with others
+ 
+ ## 4. Acceptable Use
+ 
+ You agree NOT to:
+ 
+ - Use the Platform for any illegal or unauthorized purpose
+ - Upload malicious content, malware, or harmful code
+ - Attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Platform or its systems
+ - Interfere with or disrupt the Platform's operation
+ - Use the Platform to harass, abuse, or harm others
+ - Violate any applicable laws or regulations
+ 
+ ## 5. Content and Data
+ 
+ - You retain ownership of any content you upload (audio files, text, configurations)
+ - You grant us a license to process your content to provide the service
+ - You are responsible for ensuring you have rights to any content you upload
+ - We may delete content that violates these terms
+ 
+ ## 6. Third-Party Services
+ 
+ The Platform integrates with third-party AI providers (OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, etc.). Your use of these services is subject to their respective terms and policies.
+ 
+ ## 7. Disclaimer of Warranties
+ 
+ THE PLATFORM IS PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND. WE DO NOT GUARANTEE:
+ 
+ - Uninterrupted or error-free service
+ - Accuracy of AI-generated outputs
+ - Fitness for any particular purpose
+ 
+ ## 8. Limitation of Liability
+ 
+ TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, WE SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING FROM YOUR USE OF THE PLATFORM.
+ 
+ ## 9. Modifications
+ 
+ We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of the Platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
+ 
+ ## 10. Termination
+ 
+ We may suspend or terminate your access to the Platform at any time for violation of these terms or for any other reason at our discretion.
+ 
+ ## 11. Governing Law
+ 
+ These terms shall be governed by the laws of India, without regard to conflict of law provisions.
+ 
+ ## 12. Contact
+ 
+ For questions about these Terms of Service, please contact the platform administrators.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ By using Pense, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service.
+ 
```

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,100 @@
- 
+ # Privacy Policy
+ 
+ **Last Updated:** January 2025
+ 
+ ## 1. Introduction
+ 
+ This Privacy Policy explains how Pense ("we", "our", "the Platform") collects, uses, and protects your information when you use our voice agent simulation and evaluation platform.
+ 
+ ## 2. Information We Collect
+ 
+ ### 2.1 Account Information
+ 
+ - **Google OAuth Data**: Name, email address, and profile picture from your Google account
+ - **Authentication Tokens**: Secure tokens to maintain your session
+ 
+ ### 2.2 User-Generated Content
+ 
+ - **Audio Files**: Voice recordings uploaded for STT evaluation
+ - **Text Content**: Transcriptions, system prompts, persona descriptions, scenarios
+ - **Agent Configurations**: Settings for STT, TTS, and LLM providers
+ - **Test Data**: Test cases, evaluation results, and simulation outputs
+ 
+ ### 2.3 Usage Data
+ 
+ - **Log Data**: IP address, browser type, access times, pages viewed
+ - **Performance Data**: API response times, error logs for debugging
+ 
+ ## 3. How We Use Your Information
+ 
+ We use your information to:
+ 
+ - Provide and maintain the Platform
+ - Process your agent configurations and run simulations
+ - Send your content to third-party AI providers for processing
+ - Improve the Platform's functionality and user experience
+ - Communicate important updates or changes
+ - Ensure security and prevent abuse
+ 
+ ## 4. Third-Party Services
+ 
+ Your data may be processed by third-party AI providers including:
+ 
+ - **Speech-to-Text**: Google, OpenAI, Deepgram, Cartesia, ElevenLabs, Sarvam
+ - **Text-to-Speech**: Google, OpenAI, Cartesia, ElevenLabs, Sarvam
+ - **Language Models**: OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Meta, Mistral, and others
+ 
+ Each provider has their own privacy policy governing how they handle data. We recommend reviewing their policies.
+ 
+ ## 5. Data Storage and Security
+ 
+ - Data is stored on secure cloud infrastructure
+ - Audio files are stored temporarily using pre-signed URLs
+ - We implement industry-standard security measures
+ - Access to data is restricted to authorized personnel
+ 
+ ## 6. Data Retention
+ 
+ - **Account Data**: Retained while your account is active
+ - **Audio Files**: Stored temporarily for evaluation processing
+ - **Simulation Results**: Retained until you delete them or your account
+ - **Logs**: Retained for a limited period for debugging purposes
+ 
+ ## 7. Your Rights
+ 
+ You have the right to:
+ 
+ - **Access**: Request a copy of your personal data
+ - **Correction**: Update or correct inaccurate information
+ - **Deletion**: Request deletion of your data and account
+ - **Export**: Download your configurations and results
+ 
+ To exercise these rights, contact the platform administrators.
+ 
+ ## 8. Cookies and Local Storage
+ 
+ We use:
+ 
+ - **Session Cookies**: To maintain your authentication state
+ - **Local Storage**: To store user preferences
+ 
+ ## 9. Children's Privacy
+ 
+ The Platform is not intended for users under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect information from children.
+ 
+ ## 10. International Data Transfers
+ 
+ Your data may be transferred to and processed in countries other than your own. By using the Platform, you consent to such transfers.
+ 
+ ## 11. Changes to This Policy
+ 
+ We may update this Privacy Policy periodically. We will notify you of significant changes through the Platform or via email.
+ 
+ ## 12. Contact Us
+ 
+ For questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or your data, please contact the platform administrators.
+ 
+ ---
+ 
+ By using Pense, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy.
+ 
```

</tool-use>
